recommendations.description: Fix 1 rule(s) with critical configuration validation issues
recommendations.impact: CRITICAL - Ensures rules correctly validate compliance requirements

critical_issues.description: Rule name mentions 'in_transit' encryption but checks EncryptionType (which is for at-rest encryption). Also has redundant check (equals KMS and exists).

current_mapping.expected_value: Should check in-transit encryption configuration, not EncryptionType

critical_issues.problem: Rule name mentions 'in_transit' encryption but checks EncryptionType (which is for at-rest encryption). Also has redundant check (equals KMS and exists).
critical_issues.impact: CRITICAL - Rule checks wrong encryption type (at-rest instead of in-transit) and has redundant logic
critical_issues.recommendation: Review Kinesis Video Streams API documentation for in-transit encryption configuration fields. Remove redundant 'exists' check. Verify if EncryptionType field can be used for in-transit or if different fields are needed.

cross_service_analysis.note: Note: All methods used are kinesisvideostreams service methods
cross_service_analysis.explanation: All python_methods (describe_stream) belong to kinesisvideostreams service. No cross-service issues found.
cross_service_analysis.recommendation: No action needed - rules correctly use kinesisvideostreams API methods

aws.kinesisvideostreams.stream.encryptio.issue: Checks KmsKeyId (encryption at rest) instead of encryption in transit configuration

summary.overall_status: FAIL - 1 out of 3 rules has critical logic issue
